title: refactor(image): fetchUpstream helper + propagate upstreamUrl in errors
author: voodoohop

gist.headline: X-Ray Vision for Errors
gist.blurb: We've refactored how our image and video models talk to their upstream backends. Less boilerplate code, and much smarter error logs that tell us exactly which server is acting up.
gist.summary: Introduced a new fetchUpstream helper to reduce boilerplate and improved error handling to propagate the exact upstream URL when a backend fails.
gist.impact: Developers get cleaner code, and our infrastructure team gets precise error logs showing exactly which upstream model server is experiencing issues.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee and the Monitor Robot inspecting data pipes or a server map. The robot's screen shows a magnifying glass or a specific server node, representing the new ability to trace exact upstream URLs in errors.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny yellow hard hat, inspecting glowing lime green (#ecf874) data pipes in a cozy server room. Next to her, a chunky Monitor Robot displays a magnifying glass over a specific server node on its screen. Soft pastel lavender and cream gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
